Put her in a team of other blue characters and have fun. 

You are still very early in the game, so feel free to experiment with different teams. Murphy Fauxborn has a special passive synergy with other lemurian characters like goliath, miryam, tulu, and faros but that don't come into play until you get pretty far into the story and unlock her arc 2 talent.

 Also leveling characters will give you pull currency, so you can't make many mistakes there.

Sure, you can do that. You could also try running winkle with gmurphy to see if you like it. Its a little unconventional, but they do have some reasonable synergies with winkle giving aliemus to your team and her discard triggering gmurphy's aftershock effects.

as a new player that just finished the tutorial how bad of an idea is to use all my pulls to get corposant (i think she looks cool and i have no idea what im doing)?

Early on, you can get away with pretty much anything until you hit the break-even point (Chapter 5 Arc 1 Normal Mode) and Corposant is actually generally fairly new player friendly too, but the kind of Comps (Discard) where you run her in can be fairly difficult to play with if you don't mind it

Common Comps for her would be something like

Corposant - Aurita - Cassiah - Horla

Or something along those lines

For her, you generally want to have somebody who could apply Weakness to help you stall out Turns and trigger Aftershock on her Cards (Pilots, base Strike & Defense - locked behind E1) which will grant Perm Strength Scaling then do a big finish with Lightning Retribution into a Target who you have afflicted Vulnerable on already

im too new for that i think, i just wanted to know if i could just get a cool looking and strong character (no idea if she is strong for me since like i said i just finished the tutorial) to push the main story for a while and actually learn how to play and if using all my pulls on her wouldn't just brick my account

She's a solid Character, but is not all too new player friendly, you'd need somebody to apply Vulnerable / Weakness if you don't have her at E2/3 and would also generally want to have a high Account Level / Characters with high Constitution so that you can stall out Turns comfortably which can be often be done via Tranquil Sea / White Stance (Shield Generation) to Scale / Permanent Strength Generation (through Pilots, base Strike / Defense - locked behind E1.) That's also not accounting wanting optimized Teams that specialize in Discard (Aftershock) specifically, and she typically doesn't want to be played with Characters who want to Retain their Cards
so its better to save my silver then and just roll on the blue deck with the basic currency like the guide says no?

The Flares Holy Spreadsheet? Rolling for Blue as a new Player is a very outdated opinion nowadays and you can pretty much run anything you want up to Chapter 5 where proper Team Building becomes more important

For example, Faint is not a C-Tier Character. She's really good for newer Players as she is a Caro Unit (Crimson Furnace = Healing,) can decrease the Strength of all Enemies, generate Shield / Counter, but the real kicker for her is that through her Exalt she can infinitely scale Death Resistance which is made easier with Embryos to generate Aliemus for her
Ultra is also not the worst Realm, but the Realm with the most diverse Amount of Supports that can be played interchangeably to be mixed with other Realms
https://docs.google.com/document/d/1THjxR9hHs4NWHqSWHHa2dHAGuE_cRGelfzXdBfCnIlM/edit?tab=t.0#heading=h.tqpdsy419iwp

content: I'd prefer that you refer to this instead for a New Player Guide, (but do keep in mind that it's currently still a Work in Progress)
oh i see yeah i have this decision paralysis that i dont know what i should do with my staring currency or if i should just play the story and maybe roll later when i hit a wall
Starting out, you cannot go wrong with pulling on all the Banners
You actually cripple yourself by focusing on only one of them at a time
Because for this game, you want to make alot of Teams
It's just that for Story Content specifically, you wanna have a Main Team to focus on to clear
Is faint that good for a new player? Maybe I'm undervaluing her, but her exalt doesn't sound that impactful during act 1. Running a chaos character feels like it would achieve a similar result
yeah im mostly playing the game because the art and story seem interesting
Yeah, she was a major life saver for me back when I first started as she often let me stall for Turns and the Death Resistance often comes as a nice fail safe to fall back on too when things go rough
I also skipped both GMurphy & Kath's Banner because I wasn't into their Playstyle all too much
So I didn't have a dedicated DPS to run
Up until Mouchette (who I really like and is currently playing) launched
I still regret doing these
how many copies for g: Murphy? and can I get her in her banner more than once? I got her and her wheel
Yeah, if you care for Meta and want to clear the higher Difficulties for later Content you can pull strategically. Otherwise, just go for whoever you enjoy playing
She has 3 Breakpoints with the final one generally being your goal as it would really revolutionize things for you. From base Copy, E2  - 2 Copies  / E3 - 3 Copies / OE - 7 Copies
like g: doll? but each tier is good?
Yeah, just like GDoll she really isn't all that reliable as a DPS up until you obtain her OE. Before then, at EO - E3, she's primarily a Support or at the very most a Sub DPS
